# Compatibility Report

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6700-XT
- Video driver version: Mesa 23.2.1, -TkG 24.0.0_devel.181120.b5bcb658b4d (tested both)
- Kernel version: 6.6.2-273-tkg-eevdf
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/kode54/d8d7bd021d6904ae23aa3788a07dfc24
- Proton version: GE-Proton8-24, also tried Proton 7.0-6

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Same alt+tab issue as everyone else, but I didn't come to report that. Primary issue is that when I'm running the game at "Fullscreen" 1920x1080 on my 3840x2160 monitor, the upscaled surface has tiling glitches. Running the game unscaled via Borderless Fullscreen (only fills top left quarter of desktop) or Windowed, there are no glitches.

Adding a video captured from the monitor. The glitch does not show up on unscaled mirrored outputs, as I was unable to capture it from a 1080p output with an HDMI capture device, and it did not appear in OBS screen capture.

https://f.losno.co/v/IMG_0492.mp4 (1080p60, AV1, 59MB)

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Humankind
- Steam AppID of the game: 1124300

## System Information
- GPU: RTX 2070
- Video driver version: nvidia 580.82.09
- Kernel version: Linux 6.16.8-arch3-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [Gist](https://gist.github.com/skulwicki/24aabc5722058a952779cab1e53f9d6d)
- Proton version: Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1124300.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22621042/steam-1124300.log)

## Symptoms
Current Proton Experimental appears to have a regression with Humankind. Launching the game plays an opening pre-rendered video. Letting the video run to completion or clicking to skip past the video see the game crash and unable to reach the main menu. The game had been running perfectly fine prior to the latest Experimental update from 9/19/25. Previous Proton versions 10.0-2(beta) and 9.0-4 are still working for me as of today.


## Reproduction
1. Launch Humankind
2. Either wait for the opening video to finish or click to skip past video
3. Game crashes before reaching main menu

- Steam AppID of the game: 1414850

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 570 Series
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.2.1 / LLVM 12.0.1
- Kernel version: 5.10.53-1-MANJARO
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com): https://gist.githubusercontent.com/chaorace/058400a5ab998d536c0dfa7e4d63114e/raw/04296288698dd4a737a30994847876c2a4aaff38/gistfile1.txt
- Proton version: Latest Proton Experimental (as of October 5th, 2021)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1414850.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7282738/steam-1414850.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Game launcher window opens and crashes to desktop just as the loading bar appears to fully complete. Repeated attempts to launch the game will result in the same issue.

## Reproduction
1. Download a fresh install of Nickelodeon All-Star Brawl
2. Install Proton Experimental, configure it as the default Compatibility Tool for the game
3. Press the "Play" button
4. Wait for crash to desktop

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Project Warlock (Demo)
- Steam AppID of the game: 893680 (Demo: 907740)

## System Information
- GPU: Nvidia 970 GTX
- Driver/LLVM version:  version 460.67
- Kernel version: 5.11.14-200.fc33.x86_64
- Link to full system information report as Gist: [GIST](https://gist.github.com/Eonfge/b11efc3f62d80b8a11b50deb07d8b9c3)
- Proton version: 6.3-2

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Log
[steam-907740.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6331836/steam-907740.log)

## Symptoms
Full system crash. The screen literally goes black and the machine power cycles.

## Reproduction
1. Start the Project Warlock Demo (907740) using Protn 6.3-2
1b. Make sure that the system is heavily taxed: The crash occurs when I run the game at 'high quality' 1440p uncapped, but when I go down to 1080p capped, the game remains stable for the duration of the Demo.
2. Enter the first combat arena and shoot some enemies
3. The crash occurs within 5 minutes

# Compatibility Report - Name of the game with compatibility issues: Infinity Nikki - Steam AppID of the game: 3164330 ## System Information - GPU: AMD Radeon™ RX 6600 - Video driver version: Mesa 25.2.6 - Kernel version: `6.17.8-300.fc43.x86_64` - Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/faelau/5bb4f5e6262c45171772948183bd8677 - Proton version: Proton Experimental ## I confirm: - [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game. - [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available. ## Symptoms The game launcher starts. If you click on play, the game instantly exits. With Proton GE the game starts, but crashes after less than one minute. Sporadically an EAC warning appears, but only with GE (the logs for GE are 30MB big, therefore I didn't upload them for now). The game worked a few months before, but only with GE. It never worked with Proton Experimental. I tried several fixes mentioned on [ProtonDB](https://www.protondb.com/app/3164330/) to, but without success. ## Reproduction Download and start the game without tinkering. The game launcher appears, then click on "Play". The game instantly exits. The game launcher starts successful: I tried with both GNOME 49 on Fedora Linux 43.20251122.0 (Silverblue) as well as KDE Plasma 6.4.5.

issue_title: Aselia the Eternal -The Spirit of Eternity Sword- (445420)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues:Aselia the Eternal -The Spirit of Eternity Sword-
- Steam AppID of the game:445420

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Vega 56/64
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.3.5
- Kernel version: 5.16.10
- Proton version:7.0 and before

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Game does not start. When pressing play it exits practically immediately.
Proton log shows game is trying to load MFC42.DLL, which it can't find. The MFC-DLL does not exist under the prefix in the expected location (system32). 

Wine does not have built-in MFC implementation according to FAQ.

## Reproduction
1. press play
2. observe exit

[steam-445420.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8117231/steam-445420.log)
